title: A Study About How Well TAK-279 Works and Its Safety in Participants With Moderate-to-severe Plaque Psoriasis During 52 Weeks of Treatment

eligibility.text: . Plaque psoriasis for at least 6 months.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a sufficient duration of disease to detect treatment effects.
This criterion helps ensure the study population is representative of patients with moderate to severe psoriasis, maximizing the likelihood of detecting a treatment effect.

eligibility.text: . Candidate for phototherapy or systemic therapy.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures participants are eligible for potential treatments, allowing for comparison with placebo and active comparator groups.
This criterion may be a feasibility concern as it could limit the study population to specific types of psoriasis, potentially impacting generalizability.
This criterion is likely included to protect patients from potential complications related to infections during treatment.

eligibility.text: . Prior exposure to TAK-279 or active comparator.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants have not previously received the drug, preventing confounding effects and ensuring safety.

eligibility.text: Other protocol defined inclusion/exclusion criteria apply.
eligibility.justification: This is a general category for any additional criteria that may impact feasibility or data quality.

endpoints.text: Percentage of Participants Achieving a Static Physician's Global Assessment (sPGA) of Clear (0) or Almost Clear (1) with a >=2-Point Decrease from Baseline at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Percentage of Participants Achieving >=75% Improvement from Baseline in Psoriasis Area and Severity Index (PASI) Score (PASI-75 Response) at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Percentage of Participants Achieving 90% Improvement from Baseline in PASI (PASI-90 Response) at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Percentage of Participants Achieving an sPGA of Clear (0) at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Percentage of Participants Achieving PASI-100 at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo

Percentage of Participants Achieving a Scalp-specific Physician's Global Assessment (ssPGA) of Clear (0) or Almost Clear (1) with a >=2-Point Decrease from Baseline at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Percentage of Participants with a Baseline Dermatology Life Quality Index (DLQI) Score >=2 who Achieve DLQI Score of 0 or 1 at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Percentage of Participants with a Baseline Psoriasis Symptoms and Signs Diary (PSSD) >=1 who Achieve Weekly Mean PSSD Symptom Score of 0 at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Change from Baseline in Nail Psoriasis Severity Index (NAPSI) at Week 16 Among Participants with Nail Involvement at Baseline Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Change from Baseline in Body Surface Area (BSA) Affected by Psoriasis at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Percent Change from Baseline in Body Surface Area (BSA) Affected by Psoriasis at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo

Percentage of Participants Achieving a Physician's Global Assessment (PGA) of the Hands and/or Feet of Clear (0) or Almost Clear (1) with a >=2-Point Decrease From Baseline at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Change from Baseline in DLQI at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo
Change from Baseline in the Short Form-36 Health Survey (SF-36) Version 2 Scores at Week 16 Comparing TAK-279 Against Placebo

adaptive_designs.description: Standard fixed-design trial. No adaptive features detected.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_endpoints: Number of Participants with Treatment-emergent Adverse Events (TEAEs) and Adverse Events of Special Interest (AESI)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)
